Focused device-name XCTest, failing full suite, focused publish fix, passing full suite, final full suite, Deno function tests, reference UI automation, hosted E2E, hosted tester readiness, release guard, creator/manager fixture UI, deploy inventory, schema contract, auth validation/restoration, focused auth state-machine XCTest, device pairing persistence XCTest, AdminShell auxiliary navigation, manager navigation XCTest, auth email delivery tests, operational script static checks, release metadata build settings, runbook consistency checks, runtime bootstrap/store configuration XCTest, reference import file-loader XCTest, Creator Profile editor route UI automation, signed-out SignInView UI automation, Today alternatives sheet UI automation, Shoot Folio scene detail UI automation, Weekly day-detail UI automation, and Reference Review edit sheet UI automation recorded as evidence rows.
Local And Hosted Regression Green; Installed TestFlight Parity Blocked
Do not declare completion. Continue expanding recovered matrix and installed TestFlight owner/creator parity.
Local and hosted backend confidence is high; overall confidence is still capped by blocked installed TestFlight creator journey.
As a returning tester, I want the app to restore a valid live session or show sign-in.
The app creates an authentication shell, restores Supabase auth when possible, activates live runtime after a paired session is available, and falls back to fixture shell when unauthenticated.
Missing live config, corrupt stored session, missing auth session, debug fixture flags.
Stored live session, missing config, forced fixture launch paths, pairing store clear/load guards, and corrupt keychain recovery contracts passed in final simulator regression.
Live config requires Supabase URL and publishable key.
Full prior matrix must be re-expanded from current source.
As an approved tester, I want to request an email code so I can sign in without a password.
The sign-in flow validates email shape, requests Supabase OTP, stores pending email, and moves to OTP entry.
Invalid email, busy state, use-different-email reset.
Current baseline verifies request/verification path, email normalization, rendered email-entry UI, service-level invalid email validation, and reset recovery.
Email must contain @ and . and no interior spaces after trimming.
Approval is enforced by backend exchange.
As an approved tester, I want OTP verification to create a live workspace session on this device.
OTP is six numeric digits. Verification calls Supabase verifyOTP, exchanges auth session, sends a current iOS device name, stores the paired session, activates live runtime, and refreshes app data.
Missing pending email, invalid OTP, expired auth session, unapproved tester, revoked member, device-session creation failure.
D-021 resolved: production default now uses current device name where available; missing pending-email verification returns a stable recovery error.
OTP must be exactly six numeric digits. Device name must be nonblank.
Uses simulator device name first, UIKit device name on main thread, then iPhone fallback.
As a signed-in tester, I want sign-out to revoke this device session and clear local access.
Sign-out calls revoke-device-session when possible, clears stored session, signs out locally, and returns to creator signed-out shell.
Revoke failure still clears local session and surfaces an error.
Covered by AuthenticationRuntimeTests, including revoke failure and remote auth events still clearing local access.
Requires device token and installation id for server revocation.
Local access clearing must not depend on server revoke success.
As Creator, I want to see today's published card and complete a practical content decision.
Today shows ready, loading, or missing published-card state. Decisions update local state, archive, cache, notifications, and live write-content when available.
No published card, repository failure, offline cache fallback, backup decisions.
Today ready state, offline cache, rendered alternatives sheet, and decision/archive behavior passed in XCTest, hosted E2E, and isolated simulator UI automation.
Published daily card is required for ready state.
Completion means a decision was made, not content was posted.

Weekly Setup And Creator Profile Editing
As Manager, I want to edit the weekly brief, setup sections, and creator profile before generation so the AI draft reflects current context.

The app saves dirty weekly brief/setup/profile edits through write-content before generation-sensitive actions, preserves existing setup sections on failures, and surfaces save errors without replacing current state.

As Manager, I want AI generation progress, partial failures, retry, and per-day regeneration to be visible and recoverable.
Generation emits progress, saves complete drafts, preserves last progress on failure, maps stable error messages, supports one-day regeneration, and protects published/cross-workspace plans.
